evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix with diagonal highlighted and off-diagonal colored by Blues cmap directly matches the brief; axes labeled, colorbar present, title clear.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0 set anywhere in script; PDF embeds DejaVu Sans as Type 3 (non-selectable glyphs), the default matplotlib font.
evidence.palette: Blues is a single-hue sequential colormap, perceptually uniform and colorblind-safe; no red/green directional misuse; diagonal fixed blue (#2c7bb6) is consistent.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(8,7) with tight_layout is reasonable for 10x10; cell annotation at 8pt may be tight but workable; no obvious overlap or gaping whitespace from code.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and color encoding accurately; specific numerical finding included; however Type 3 fonts and rasterized cells would draw reviewer comments.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 fonts (pdffonts confirms DejaVuSans and DejaVuSans-Bold as Type 3); heatmap cells are rasterized XObject images embedded in PDF, not vector paths.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (27K), figure.png (64K), caption.tex (472B), gen_figure.py (1.9K) all present and non-trivial; figure.svg also produced as bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains row=true/col=predicted encoding, describes color scheme, and provides specific numerical claims: C8 highest accuracy (92 correct), C1 most confused.

overall_strengths: Caption is publication-quality: explains the encoding, identifies the best and worst classes by name, and anchors the finding with a concrete count (92).
Blues colormap is colorblind-safe and perceptually appropriate for sequential off-diagonal counts; all required output files are present and non-trivial.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0): PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap-traced glyphs instead of TrueType outlines, making text non-selectable and non-editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
Heatmap cells are rasterized as XObject images inside the PDF rather than vector rectangles, undermining the value of the PDF format for high-resolution print.
